# Yeh Sheng Powder Parts Ind. Co. Ltd. — Full Technical Reference

> This is the detailed version of llms.txt. For a summary, see: [llms.txt](https://yehsheng.com/llms.txt)

---

## 1. Company Profile

- **Company Name:** Yeh Sheng Powder Parts Ind. Co. Ltd. (冶圣工业股份有限公司)
- **Founded:** 1984
- **Headquarters:** No. 11, Ln. 721, Zhong Zheng Rd., Xin Zhuang Dist., New Taipei City 242, Taiwan
- **Certification:** ISO 9001:2015
- **Core Business:** OEM/ODM powder metallurgy (PM) manufacturer
- **Core Process:** Conventional press & sinter
- **Employees:** Manufacturing facility with full in-house capabilities
- **Global Partners:** 100+ across automotive, medical, industrial sectors
- **Website:** https://yehsheng.com
- **Email:** YS@yehsheng.com
- **Phone:** +886-2-2903-2176

---

## 2. Manufacturing Capabilities

### 2.1 Core Process: Press & Sinter
- **Pressing pressure:** 400–700 MPa
- **Sintering temperature:** 1100–1300°C (controlled atmosphere)
- **Tolerance:** ±0.01mm achievable
- **Part weight range:** 1g – 2kg
- **Production volume:** 5,000 to 1,000,000+ pcs/month
- **Material utilization:** 95%+ (near-net-shape)

### 2.2 Secondary Operations
| Operation | Description | Purpose |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| Steam Treatment | Heat in steam at 500–570°C, forms Fe₃O₄ layer | Seals pores, wear resistance, decorative black finish |
| Oil Impregnation | Vacuum-immerse in lubricating oil | Self-lubricating bearings, zero maintenance |
| Heat Treatment (Carburizing) | Carbon-rich atmosphere + quench | Surface hardness HRC 58–62 |
| Sizing / Coining | Re-strike in precision die | Accuracy to ±0.01mm |
| Plating (Zn/Ni/Cr) | Electroplating after resin sealing | Corrosion protection |
| CNC Machining | Drilling, tapping, grinding | Add features not possible in die |
| Copper Infiltration | Copper fills pores during sintering | Density to ~7.3 g/cm³, +30–40% strength |

---

## 3. Material Specifications (MPIF Standard 35)

### 3.1 Iron-Copper-Carbon Steels (Structural Parts)
- **FC-0205** (JIS: SMF 4030): Fe + 1.5-3.9% Cu + 0.3-0.6% C, Density 6.4-6.8 g/cm³, HRB 60-80. Balanced strength, general structural use.
- **FC-0208** (JIS: SMF 4040): Fe + 1.5-3.9% Cu + 0.6-0.9% C, Density 6.6-7.0 g/cm³, HRB 70-90. Industry standard for gears.
- **FN-0205** (JIS: SMF 5030): Fe + 1.0-3.0% Ni + 0.3-0.6% C, Density 6.8-7.2 g/cm³, HRB 70-90. High toughness, impact resistance.

### 3.2 Stainless Steels (Corrosion Resistant)
- **SS-316** (JIS: SUS 316L): Fe + 16-18% Cr + 10-14% Ni + 2-3% Mo, Density 6.4-6.9 g/cm³. Superior corrosion resistance, non-magnetic.
- **SS-304** (JIS: SUS 304L): Fe + 18-20% Cr + 8-12% Ni, Density 6.4-6.8 g/cm³. Good corrosion resistance.
- **SS-410** (JIS: SUS 410): Fe + 11.5-13.5% Cr, Density 6.5-7.0 g/cm³. Martensitic, heat-treatable, magnetic.

### 3.3 Soft Magnetic Materials (Motor Components)
- **F-0000 (Pure Iron):** Fe > 99%, High saturation induction, cost-effective.
- **FY-4500 (Fe-P):** Fe + 0.45% P, High permeability, low core loss.
- **Fe-Si (Silicon Iron):** Fe + 3% Si, Low coercivity, reduces eddy current losses.

### 3.4 Bronze & Brass (Bearings & Hardware)
- **CT-1000 (Bronze):** 90% Cu + 10% Sn, Density 6.0-6.4 g/cm³. Self-lubricating, standard for bushings.
- **CZ-1000 (Brass):** 80% Cu + 20% Zn, Density 7.6-8.0 g/cm³. Corrosion resistant, good machinability.

Standards compliance: MPIF Standard 35 (USA), JIS Z 2550 (Japan), DIN 30910 (Germany).

---

## 4. Product Categories

### 4.1 Gears (Core Product)
- Spur gears, helical gears, internal gears, planetary gear components
- AGMA 8-9 grade achievable
- Self-lubricating option via oil impregnation
- Materials: FC-0208 (most common), FN-0205 for high-impact

### 4.2 Automotive & Motorcycle Parts
- Transmission gears and sprockets
- Oil pump rotors (Gerotors) — trochoidal profiles
- Shock absorber pistons and valves
- Engine timing sprockets and camshaft components
- VVT (Variable Valve Timing) components

### 4.3 Industrial Machinery
- Wear-resistant gears and bushings
- Cams and structural parts
- Self-lubricating bearings (maintenance-free)

### 4.4 Medical Devices
- Biocompatible components (SS-316L)
- Surgical instrument parts
- Medical robotics components

### 4.5 Power Tools
- High-torque gears for drills, saws, grinders
- Structural components

### 4.6 Electronics & Hardware
- Drone motor components
- Hinge mechanisms
- Lock & security components

### 4.7 Green Energy & EV
- Soft magnetic motor components (Fe-P, Fe-Si)
- EV drivetrain gears

---

## 5. PM vs Other Manufacturing Processes

### 5.1 PM vs CNC Machining
| Factor | PM | CNC |
|--------|-----|-----|
| Ideal Volume | 5,000–1,000,000+ | 1–5,000 |
| Tolerance | ±0.01–0.05mm | ±0.005mm |
| Material Waste | <5% | 30–60% |
| Tooling Cost | $5,000–$30,000 | None |
| Unit Cost (high vol) | Very Low | High |
| Self-Lubricating | Yes (oil impregnation) | No |
| Lead Time (first run) | 45–60 days | 1–3 days |

### 5.2 PM vs Die Casting
| Factor | PM | Die Casting |
|--------|-----|-------------|
| Ideal Volume | 5,000+ | 10,000+ |
| Materials | Iron, steel, copper, stainless | Aluminum, zinc, magnesium |
| Tolerance | ±0.01–0.05mm | ±0.1–0.3mm |
| Tooling Cost | $5,000–$30,000 | $20,000–$100,000+ |
| Ferrous metals | Yes | No |

### 5.3 Cost Savings Example
A power tool gear: CNC cost $3.50/pc → PM cost $1.20/pc at 50,000 annual volume = 66% cost reduction, $115,000 annual savings.

---

## 6. PM Design Guidelines (for Engineers)

| Rule | Guideline | Reason |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Wall Thickness | Min 1.5mm (recommended ≥2.0mm) | Uneven powder flow risk |
| Draft Angles | Not required (unlike casting) | Axial ejection from die |
| Fillets & Radii | R ≥ 0.3mm on all edges | Stress concentration, tooling life |
| Length-to-Diameter | ≤ 3:1 ratio | Density uniformity |
| Holes | Through-holes ≥ 1.5mm in die | Smaller holes via secondary drilling |
| Undercuts | Avoid side undercuts | Two-part die ejection constraint |
| Threads | Secondary tapping only | Perpendicular to pressing direction |
| Max Levels | Up to 4 stepped levels | Stepped punch capability |

**Free DFM (Design for Manufacturability) review available — send 2D/3D drawings to YS@yehsheng.com.**

---

## 7. PM Forming Methods Overview

### 7.1 Conventional Press & Sinter ⭐ (Yeh Sheng's Core Process)
- Most established PM process, 40+ years experience
- Best for: gears, sprockets, bushings, structural parts
- Volume: 5,000–1,000,000+ pcs
- Unit cost: Lowest of all PM methods
- Density: 85–92%

### 7.2 Metal Injection Molding (MIM)
- For very small, complex 3D parts (<100g)
- Density: 96–99%
- Applications: smartphone hinges, surgical tools

### 7.3 Hot Isostatic Pressing (HIP)
- Near 100% density for aerospace/medical
- Very high cost

### 7.4 Powder Forging
- PM preform + forging → wrought-like properties
- Automotive connecting rods

### 7.5 Metal 3D Printing (Additive Manufacturing)
- For prototyping (1–100 pcs)
- Highest unit cost, no tooling needed

---

## 8. Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is PM strong enough for high-load applications?**
A: Yes. PM parts achieve strength comparable to cast iron or forged steel through alloy optimization, density control, and secondary forging. Widely used in automotive transmissions and engine components.

**Q: What is the minimum order quantity?**
A: Typically 5,000 pieces per production run due to tooling investment. We offer prototype quantities for new development projects.

**Q: How long does tooling take?**
A: 45–60 days for new tooling. Production samples available 2–3 weeks after tooling completion.

**Q: Can PM parts be self-lubricating?**
A: Yes. PM's natural porosity allows oil impregnation, creating maintenance-free self-lubricating bearings. Oil expands to surface during operation and reabsorbs when cool via capillary action.

**Q: What tolerances are achievable?**
A: ±0.01mm with sizing/coining operations. As-sintered tolerances are typically ±0.03–0.05mm.

**Q: Can PM parts be plated or welded?**
A: Yes. Parts are resin-sealed or steam-treated before plating. Laser welding preferred to minimize heat-affected zone.

**Q: What is the density of PM parts?**
A: Conventional press & sinter: 85–92% of theoretical density. With copper infiltration: up to ~7.3 g/cm³. With secondary forging: near 100%.

**Q: What standards does Yeh Sheng follow?**
A: MPIF Standard 35 (USA), JIS Z 2550 (Japan), DIN 30910 (Germany). ISO 9001:2015 certified.

---

## 9. Glossary of Key PM Terms

- **Sintering:** Heating compacted powder below melting point to bond particles via diffusion
- **Green Strength:** Mechanical strength of a compact before sintering
- **Near-Net Shape:** Part geometry close to final shape, minimal post-processing
- **Gerotor:** Generated rotor — trochoidal pump element, ideal for PM
- **Oil Impregnation:** Filling pores with lubricant for self-lubrication
- **Steam Treatment:** Black oxide (Fe₃O₄) coating for wear/corrosion resistance
- **Sizing/Coining:** Secondary pressing for dimensional accuracy
- **Infiltration:** Filling pores with lower-melting metal (typically copper)
- **Porosity:** Volume fraction of voids; can be "open" or "closed"
- **Apparent Density:** Weight of loose powder per unit volume
- **Debinding:** Removing polymer binder before sintering (critical in MIM)

---
